metadata.loginPostLandingElement: Authenticated header showing user email 'manish123@gmail.com', nav links (Home, Events, My Bookings, API Docs, Admin), and Logout button.
metadata.notes: Credentials read from src/tests/steps/test.ts but never hardcoded in this output — referenced only as 'email' and 'password' field names per task constraints.

addEventEntryPoint.location: Top of the /admin/events page (inline form, always visible) — reachable from two places while logged in
addEventEntryPoint.primaryLabel: + Add Event (submit button on the inline form)

formHeading.note: The '+ New Event' text near the form is a static section heading, NOT a clickable toggle.

waysToReachTheForm.result: Form is rendered at the top of the page

addEventEntryPoint.formSelector: form#admin-event-form   [data-testid="admin-event-form"]   (novalidate attribute — uses custom error rendering)
addEventEntryPoint.eventLimit: You can add up to 6 events. Once the limit is reached, your oldest event is automatically replaced when you add a new one. (Up to 9 rows are visible in the management table including the 3 'Featured' seeded events; user-created events are capped at 6.)

formFields.name: title
formFields.label: Title
formFields.validationHint: Label has red asterisk. On empty submit: 'Title is required' (red text under field). HTML5 required attr is present but form is novalidate so custom error wins.

formFields.name: description
formFields.label: Description
formFields.selector: textarea[placeholder="Describe the event…"]   (the only textarea in the form — has no id/name)
formFields.validationHint: Optional. No validation message ever shown for this field.

formFields.name: category
formFields.label: Category
formFields.validationHint: Label has red asterisk. A value is preselected so empty-submit never flags this field — but the label visually marks it required. Custom validation cannot be observed on this field from the current form.

formFields.name: dateTime
formFields.label: Event Date & Time
formFields.selector: input[type="datetime-local"] (only one in form — id is 'event-date-&-time')
formFields.validationHint: On empty submit: 'Event date is required'. On past-date submit: 'Must be a future date'. (Confirmed by manual submit of '2020-01-15T10:00'.)

formFields.name: price
formFields.label: Price ($)
formFields.validationHint: On empty submit: 'Enter a valid price (≥ 0)'. On negative value submit (-50): 'Enter a valid price (≥ 0)'.

formFields.name: totalSeats
formFields.label: Total Seats
formFields.validationHint: On empty submit: 'Must have at least 1 seat'. On zero submit: 'Must have at least 1 seat' (HTML5 min=1 would block it, but the form is novalidate so the custom error takes over).

formFields.name: imageUrl
formFields.label: Image URL (optional)
formFields.validationHint: Optional. On invalid URL submit ('not-a-url') the form rejects the value with no inline error visible — server returns 400 (visible in console). The event is not added to the list.

testCases.title: Happy path — create event with all required fields and verify it appears in the list
preconditions: User is logged in (email/password from src/tests/steps/test.ts)
User has navigated to /admin/events (inline form is visible)

steps.value: A great QA conference covering automation, accessibility, and AI.

testCases.expectedResult: Event 'QA Conference 2026' is added to the All Events table on /admin/events and is also browsable on /events. Form fields are reset to empty after submission. No toast/banner is shown — the only positive feedback is the row appearing in the table.
testCases.executionNotes: Confirmed live: row with 'QA Conference 2026' / Conference / Bangalore / 15 Aug 2027 / $499 / 500/500 appeared in the admin table and in the /events listing. Title input value re-read as '' after submit, confirming form reset. No console errors during submission.

testCases.title: Required-field validation — submit empty form, expect per-field error messages
preconditions: User is logged in and on /admin/events
testCases.expectedResult: Submission is blocked. Inline error text appears under each required field. Observed messages: 'Title is required' (under title), 'City is required' (under city), 'Venue is required' (under venue), 'Event date is required' (under date), 'Enter a valid price (≥ 0)' (under price), 'Must have at least 1 seat' (under seats). Description and Image URL have no error (optional). Category is preselected so it cannot appear empty. No event row is added to the table; URL remains /admin/events.
testCases.executionNotes: Confirmed live: all six required-field error messages rendered in red (text-red-500/600/700) on empty submit. Table row count unchanged (9 → 9). The form has the HTML attribute novalidate, which is why the browser-native 'Please fill out this field' is not shown — the app renders its own messages instead.

testCases.title: Boundary / invalid data — past date, zero seats, negative price, invalid image URL
preconditions: User is logged in and on /admin/events

subCases.name: Invalid image URL
subCases.expectedError: No inline UI error visible; server returns HTTP 400 and the event is not added. (UX risk: silent rejection — user gets no clear feedback.)

testCases.expectedResult: Each invalid submission is rejected. The All Events table does not grow. For past date / zero seats / negative price, a clear inline error appears. For invalid image URL, the rejection is silent on the UI (only visible as a 400 in the browser console) — recommend this be flagged as a UX defect.
testCases.executionNotes: All four boundary cases confirmed live in the same session. The table row count stayed at 9 throughout — none of the boundary inputs created a row. Console error 'Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 400 (Bad Request)' was emitted only during the invalid-URL attempt.

testCases.title: Optional fields — submit a minimal event (no description, no image) and a fully-populated event; both should persist
preconditions: User is logged in and on /admin/events

fields.title: Full Optional Event
fields.description: Event with all optional fields populated for verification.

steps.expected: Both 'Minimal Event Test' and 'Full Optional Event' should appear in the admin table and on /events.

testCases.expectedResult: Both events are created. Price=0 is accepted. Missing description and image URL do not block submission and the events still appear in the listing.
testCases.executionNotes: Both rows observed in the admin table on /admin/events and in the public /events listing. This confirms description and imageUrl are truly optional and that price=0 is allowed.

testCases.title: Cancel / back navigation — start filling the form, navigate away, confirm form state and absence of a Cancel button
preconditions: User is logged in and on /admin/events
testCases.expectedResult: There is no Cancel, Reset, or Clear button on the form (verified by selectors button:has-text('Cancel|Reset|Clear|Discard') — all return 0 matches). The only ways to leave the form are: (a) submit, which clears the form on success, (b) click a header/footer link, or (c) browser back. After navigating away and pressing browser back, the form is empty (state is not preserved across navigation).
testCases.executionNotes: Live: after clicking the EventHub header logo (→ /) and pressing browser back, the form's title input was empty. No 'unsaved changes' confirmation prompt is offered. UX risk: if a user accidentally navigates away mid-entry, they lose their data silently. Recommend adding a Cancel/Discard button and/or a beforeunload confirmation.

observations: Entry-point confusion: there are TWO distinct elements that read as 'Add New Event'. (1) A clickable button on /events (anchored at the bottom of the listing) that navigates to /admin/events. (2) A static <h2> '+ New Event' heading at the top of the form on /admin/events that is NOT interactive. The form is always visible — there is no toggle. The actual submit button is labeled '+ Add Event' (id=add-event-btn). This may confuse automation: prefer targeting #add-event-btn for submit, and use the header link as the navigation trigger if a 'click to open form' step is needed.

Form uses novalidate, so HTML5 validation is suppressed in favor of app-level error rendering. Errors appear in red text below the affected field (text-red-500/600/700). Empty submit triggers 6 inline error messages: 'Title is required', 'City is required', 'Venue is required', 'Event date is required', 'Enter a valid price (≥ 0)', 'Must have at least 1 seat'.

Field IDs include special characters (& and $), which makes them brittle to use as CSS selectors. The robust strategy is to use selectors like input[type='datetime-local'], input[type='number']:nth-of-type(1/2), input[type='url'], and the data-testid attributes that the app already provides (event-title-input, category, city, venue, total-seats, add-event-btn, admin-event-form).

No success toast or confirmation banner is shown after a successful submit — the only positive feedback is the new row appearing in the All Events table. (The .text-emerald-600 selectors only ever match seat counts like '481/3000' or '500/500', never a toast.) This is a minor UX gap.
Form auto-clears all fields after a successful submission (title input value re-read as ''), which is the correct behavior.

Category select is preselected to 'Conference' and has no `required` attribute, so empty-form submit never surfaces a category error — but the label has the red '*', so users will still perceive it as required. Automation should treat category as required regardless.

Event limit / rotation policy: 'You can add up to 6 events. Once the limit is reached, your oldest event is automatically replaced when you add a new one.' Live behaviour matched this — after creating several events, the table row count stayed at 9 (3 featured seeded + 6 user-created) and the oldest user-created event was evicted. The /events listing showed the same rotation. Automation that asserts 'row count grows by 1' will fail past the 7th user-created event.

Invalid image URL produces no inline UI feedback — only an HTTP 400 from the API surfaces in the browser console. Recommend the app surface a clear 'Image URL must be a valid URL' error. Filed this as a UX defect inside TC3.

No Cancel/Reset/Discard button is rendered anywhere on the form. The only ways to leave the form mid-entry are submit or navigate (header link / browser back). Browser-back loses the in-progress data silently — recommend a confirmation prompt or a Cancel button. Filed this in TC5.

Accessibility red flags observed: form fields have label[for=id] associations, but the IDs contain characters (& and $) that assistive tech and many CSS selectors handle poorly. Required status is communicated only via a red '*' sibling in the label, with no aria-required attribute and no programmatic link between the '*' and the requirement. Description textarea has no id at all and no associated label — its label is implicit via proximity. Image URL label includes the word 'optional' in the label text itself rather than using aria-describedby. There are no visible focus rings beyond the default browser outline.

Console errors observed during the initial homepage load: several 'Failed to fetch RSC payload … Falling back to browser navigation' messages and two 404s on /addEvent and /add-event probes (probes I issued; not user-driven). The RSC fallback messages are harmless but noisy; they appear because the test script did multiple in-page navigations. One genuine server-side error observed during TC3: HTTP 400 when submitting the bad-image-URL case.
testDataNotes: Credentials (email, password) were read from src/tests/steps/test.ts but are NOT embedded in this JSON — they are referenced by field name only per the task constraints.

All test data used in form fills was synthetic (event titles like 'QA Conference 2026', 'Minimal Event Test', 'Full Optional Event', cities like 'Bangalore'/'Pune'/'Delhi', dates from 2027). No real customer data was entered.

Screenshots captured for evidence during exploration are stored alongside this JSON file: _login.png, _post-login.png, _admin-events.png, _filled-happy.png, _after-happy-submit.png, _empty-submit.png, _past-date.png, _optional-empty.png, _optional-filled.png, _final-admin.png.
